Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3683

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 548, "B93": 154, "A11": 60, "B33": 517, "C08": 334, "B39": 542, "B96": 707, "C85": 139, "A82": 661, "B31": 780, "B20": 832, "A60": 913, "B09": 219, "C81": 323, "B21": 638}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.

But they propose different set; we need either AGREE on opponent's proposal, or REFUSE and/or make our new proposal. Must use structured format and only one message. Also need to include ARGUMENT at least once.

We cannot violate effort limit: let's compute for their set:

Items: A11(60), B09(219), B20(832), B39(542), B93(154), B96(707), C81(323), C85(139), A21(548). Sum = 60+219=279; +832=110... 279+832=1111? wait recalc: 60+219=279, +832=1111, +542=1653, +154=1807, +707=2514, +323=2837, +139=2976, +548=3524. 3524 <= 3683; so within limit.

That's far higher. But limit check: 3164 <=3683 okay. So our set has value 5822 >> opponent's 3555. So we have better. Our goal maximize personal importance. So we would prefer to keep our own set and refuse opponent's proposal. We could also propose our set again. But we already proposed that earlier. They might have refused or not? In last message they didn't REFUSE. Our previous message had PROPOSAL. They didn't respond. We can continue negotiating. So we can REFUSE their proposal, and maybe propose ours again, or propose a new set maybe with more value? Our set 5822 is already optimal relative to items within limit. But maybe we can add B31 again? Already in set. Could we swap for A82 or B20? Let's estimate: A82 importance 508 but effort 661. Replacing B31 (780 effort, importance 780) with A82 would reduce importance and possibly leave slack. We might add B20 832 effort, imp461 maybe combine with others. Our set has 9 items. Could add A82 instead of B31? Let's compute effect: Remove B31: remove 780 importance, 780 effort -> new effort 2384? Wait previous effort 3164. Remove 780 = 2384. Add A82 661 => 3045. We can still add others maybe: B20 832? 3045 +832 = 3877 > limit, not possible. Add B20 plus perhaps something else? Too high. Add A82 and B20? too high. Could add B20 and keep B31? 3164+832=3996>limit. So cannot add both. Our set seems near optimum. Could add B20 by dropping something else like C85 (121 importance). Removing C85 139 effort, 121 importance. Then effort 3164-139=3025. Add B20 832 => 3857>limit. Add A82 661 => 3025+661=3686>limit . So can't add those. Maybe add B20 by dropping B21 (808 importance 638 effort). Removal 638 => 3164-638=2526. Add B20 832 => 3358 < limit. But lost 808 importance, gained 461 => net -347. So less. So our current set is quite high importance.

Thus best to REJECT opponent's proposal and press our own. So respond with REFUSE and possibly propose our set again or a new one if better. We can just REFUSE and AGREE? No cannot. We can propose again. So we produce:

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.

Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

raw_response_obj.cot_content:
The user’s last message: They propose a set of items, and they refuse the assistant's current proposal. This is the last message. The game ends when you agree to a proposal made by the other player. So we could choose to accept the user's proposal by offering AGREE to their proposal set. Must be within limit.

We need to check if user set is within limit. Items: A11 (60 effort), B33 (517), B21 (638), C08 (334), B93 (154), B09 (219), C81 (323), B31 (780), A21 (548). Sum effort: 60+517=577, +638=1215, +334=1549, +154=1703, +219=1922, +323=2245, +780=3025, +548=3573. That's 3573, within limit 3683. So we can agree.

In the protocol we need AGREE format. We must give that message. Provide set exactly as offered. Must be the set from the last proposal from opponent: {'A11', 'B33', 'B21', 'C08', 'B93', 'B09', 'C81', 'B31', 'A21'}.

We can also optionally give ARGUMENT? According to interaction protocol: The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty. Strictly following? The tags should be structured. The message can be AGREE. Possibly we can also give an ARGUMENT? The example says we must include ARGUMENT at least once somewhere in all messages. We already have provided arguments before. So we can just produce

AGREE: {'A11', 'B33', 'B21', 'C08', 'B93', 'B09', 'C81', 'B31', 'A21'}

Thus final.

ftext|><|start|>system<|message|>You Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3683

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 548, "B93": 154, "A11": 60, "B33": 517, "C08": 334, "B39": 542, "B96": 707, "C85": 139, "A82": 661, "B31": 780, "B20": 832, "A60": 913, "B09": 219, "C81": 323, "B21": 638}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.


You will now receive the first message of the other player.
